macro_segments.title: Idolatry That Breaks On Its Own: The Debate Between Rabbi Yochanan and Reish Lakish

micro_segments.title: Introduction to the Dispute: Does an Idol Retain Its Forbidden Status When It Breaks Naturally?
Rabbi Yochanan's Position: The Idol Remains Forbidden Without Conscious Negation
Reish Lakish's Position: The Idol Becomes Permitted Because It Failed to Save Itself

macro_segments.title: The Proof from Bird's Nests in Trees: Reconciling the Mishna with the Dispute Over Idol Status

micro_segments.title: Reish Lakish's Challenge: How Can You Benefit from a Nest Made with Twigs from an Asheira Tree?
The Gemara's Response: The Twigs Must Come from Other Trees, Not from the Asheira Itself
Alternative Reading from Avoda Zara: Why You Must Use a Stick Rather Than Taking the Nest Directly
Tosafot's Explanation: Using a Stick Prevents Accidentally Taking Branches of the Tree Itself

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Yochanan's Concession: Even If Twigs Come from the Tree, You Can Benefit from the Chicks

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Abba in the Name of Rabbi Yochanan: Distinguishing Between the Nest (Forbidden) and the Chicks (Permitted)
The Status of Eggs and Young Birds: Eggs Are Forbidden, Chicks That Need Their Mother Are Treated Like the Nest
Rav Ashi's Qualification: Chicks Needing Their Mother Are Like the Eggs and Part of the Nest

macro_segments.title: Treasurers Purchasing Wood for the Temple: Distinguishing Between the Beam and the Sawdust

micro_segments.title: The Mishna: Treasurers' Purchased Wood Incurs Meila, But Not the Sawdust or Shavings
The Distinction Between Kedushat Haguf and Kedushat Damim: Forest vs. Individual Beams for Building

macro_segments.title: Building with Non-Sanctified Materials and Then Sanctifying: The Rule and Its Rationale

micro_segments.title: Shmuel's Rule: Build with Unsanctified Items and Only Sanctify After Construction Is Complete
The Explanation: Transferring Sanctity from Money to Completed Structure via Workers' Payment
The Mechanism: Separating Worker Payment from Sanctified Funds So Sanctity Transfers to the Structure
Challenge: Why Not Just Transfer Sanctity Directly to the Structure Without Using Worker Funds?
Rav Pappa's Reason: Raw Materials Must Be Unsanctified to Prevent Accidental Meila During Construction

macro_segments.title: Applying the Rule to the Mishna: Why Does the Treasurer's Wood Incur Meila If It Should Be Unsanctified?

micro_segments.title: The Gemara's Question: If We Build with Unsanctified Materials, Why Is There Meila?
Rav Pappa's Resolution: We Are Discussing Materials for Immediate Use, Not Long-Term Storage

macro_segments.title: Supplementary Discussion: Meila by the Wool and Horns of a Korban

micro_segments.title: Incidental Note: Horns of Shelamim and Wool of Olah Both Incur Meila When Used

topical_tags.term: Kedushat Haguf vs. Kedushat Damim (Form vs. Value Sanctity)
